threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Overground' instead of just 'the train.' Locals know the real vibe is that it's more than a route.
Last night on the 59 by Brixton Market, a girl started handing out homemade cupcakes. Only in South London, right? They were surprisingly good.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: One afternoon, I saw three generations of families sharing a kebab stand while kids played nearby. That’s when it hit me—this place is alive.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You'll see folks in Kreuzberg braving the cold with outdoor karaoke setups. It's not just a party; it's a midwinter tradition that keeps the spirit alive.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they don’t flinch at the Muni delays but can rattle off three taco spots in the same breath.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, saw a guy serenading commuters with a ukulele at 16th St. BART—only in the Mission can you catch a live show while waiting for the F-line.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell, but it's the local parks like Plaça de la Virreina that have the real vibe. They miss the casual evening life here.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still take photos of the street performers on every corner, oblivious to the city's soundtrack blending in with their daily lives.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still refer to Clissold Park as a 'hidden gem' instead of just the meeting point for every kid's birthday party.
Last night on the 333, I saw a guy in a full tuxedo arguing with a mannequin he named 'Veronica'. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy repairing his bike while a woman juggled oranges at the corner stand—only in Neukölln do you get that level of multitasking.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can catch a drag queen performing in a laundromat here. Only in Neukölln will you find the mundane turned fabulous.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by their ability to navigate Muni's unpredictable schedule without a second thought—like it's part of their DNA.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 14 bus riders with an accordion yesterday. The Muni vibe is something else—only in the Mission can you get a free concert while waiting!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that locals don’t queue for xurros at the popular spots—it's all about the hidden gems in the side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still trying to order a 'café con leche' without stumbling over pronunciation, looking at the menu like it’s a crossword puzzle.
